headline: NEP Achieved 23% Bumiputera Equity — Target Was 30% by 1990
context: The New Economic Policy's 30% Bumiputera equity target, set for 1990, has never been met. Government figures show 23% in 2025, though methodology is disputed.

cards.big: The NEP set a 30% Bumiputera equity target for 1990.
cards.sub: 35 years past the deadline, the figure stands at 23%. The policy has outlived its timeline by a generation.

cards.big: UNCTAD counts equity at market value: Bumiputera share is 23%.
cards.sub: EPU uses par value: Bumiputera share is 19.4%. The two methods disagree by RM180B.

cards.big: Top 20% of Bumiputera households own 72% of Bumiputera equity.
cards.sub: The NEP's equity gains concentrate at the top, not across the community it claims to uplift.

cards.big: Malaysia's Gini coefficient for Bumiputera is 0.39 — higher than non-Bumiputera at 0.37.
cards.sub: Intra-Bumiputera inequality has widened under the policy designed to address inter-ethnic inequality.

cards.big: A policy meant to reduce inequality between races has increased inequality within the race it was meant to help.

The NEP's stated goal was restructuring society. By its own metrics, it has not succeeded after 55 years. The question is whether the current approach can achieve what it has not achieved, or whether a new framework is needed.
